summary.intent: Extract HTTP server functionality into a dedicated `httpd` crate from the `gateway` crate, merge main repeatedly to stay current, and address code review feedback on PR #465.
summary.outcome: The httpd crate extraction is complete and the PR is ready to merge; all greptile review comments across multiple rounds were fixed and resolved, formatting/test failures were corrected, and logs.txt was purged from git history.

repo: The `moltis-httpd` crate owns HTTP transport (axum, tower-http, rustls, tokio-tungstenite) while `moltis-gateway` owns business logic (sessions, RPC, channels, agents, auth); dependency is strictly one-way: httpd → gateway.
rustls crypto provider `install_default()` must be called in `prepare_gateway` (not `start_gateway`) so both the direct and swift-bridge/embedded startup paths initialise TLS correctly.

`browser_tool_for_warmup` was silently dropped by a `..` destructure pattern in `PreparedGatewayCore`; it must be extracted explicitly and threaded through `BannerMeta` to reach `start_browser_warmup_after_listener`.
`_proxy_shutdown_tx` must be kept alive inside the spawn closure in swift-bridge; dropping it at destructure time kills the trusted-network proxy immediately.
The metrics collection interval is 30 s (120 ticks × 30 s = 1 h cleanup cycle); accidentally setting it to 10 s shrinks the history window from 6 h to 2 h.

The `install` feature gate on `moltis-skills/src/lib.rs` was dead code on main (feature never defined in Cargo.toml); main subsequently added unconditional users of the module, so the gate must remain absent.
The `/ws` route alias must exist alongside `/ws/chat` so node-add generated commands continue to work.
Session event enrichment (label, model, preview, channel binding) was ported from gateway to httpd; without it clients receive only `kind` + `sessionKey`.
The `cron_service` field on `PreparedGateway` requires callers to explicitly call `.start()` — this was undocumented and flagged by review.
4 release jobs each had a redundant pre-version build step (compiled binary before `MOLTIS_VERSION` was set, then compiled again with it), wasting CI time.
The sandboxes E2E test failures (missing 'Refresh' button) are pre-existing on main and unrelated to this PR — they reflect container runtime unavailability in the test environment.
A merge conflict resolution incorrectly chose `!(isMain || isCron)` instead of main's `!isMain` in `session-header.js`, hiding the delete button for cron sessions.

code.finding: Central file for the extraction; owns prepare_gateway, start_gateway, BannerMeta, PreparedGateway structs, session event enrichment loop, and metrics collection interval constant.
start_browser_warmup_after_listener signature was updated to accept the browser tool parameter; PreparedGatewayCore fields that were silently dropped via `..` now need explicit extraction.
Local `MAX_LIFETIME` constant was shadowed by module-level `MAX_BROWSER_INSTANCE_LIFETIME`; the local was unused and should be removed.
Systemd ExecStart arguments (--node-id, --host, --token, display name, working dir) and launchd plist values must be quoted/xml-escaped to handle spaces and special characters.
Duplicate /api/sessions route registrations would panic axum at startup; one set must be removed.
Delete button visibility guard should be `!isMain` not `!(isMain || isCron)`; cron sessions are deletable per the test suite.
moltis-agents must be added as a workspace dependency for httpd to reference the AgentTool type when threading browser_tool_for_warmup through BannerMeta.

After moving gateway tests to httpd/tests/, imports must change from `moltis_gateway::{build_gateway_base, finalize_gateway_app}` to `moltis_httpd::server::{...}`; nightly rustfmt also requires grouped use blocks.
_proxy_shutdown_tx must be moved into the spawn closure, not held in the outer scope where it gets dropped before the async task runs.

workflow: git-filter-repo removes the `origin` remote as a safety measure after rewriting history; always re-add it with `git remote add origin <url>` before force-pushing.
Force-pushing rewritten history closes open GitHub PRs because GitHub sees the branch as entirely new; use `gh pr reopen <id>` or create a new PR.

When git-filter-repo rewrites the entire repo history (not just branch commits), the rewritten branch shares no ancestor with origin/main; the fix is to create a new branch from origin/main and apply the diff as a squash commit.
gh CLI requires a default repo set (`gh repo set-default`) when the remote was removed and re-added manually.
greptile review comments are retrievable via `gh api` on pull request review threads; resolving them requires PATCH requests to mark threads as resolved.
nightly rustfmt enforces grouped `use` blocks — separate `use moltis_gateway::...` and `use moltis_httpd::...` statements must be merged into a single `use { ... }` block.
Cargo.lock conflicts should be resolved by taking main's version then running `cargo fetch` rather than attempting manual merge.

friction: git-filter-repo silently removed the origin remote, causing the force-push to require manual remote re-add and then unexpectedly closing the PR.
Rewriting entire repo history (2027 commits) with filter-repo broke the branch's relationship with main, requiring a squash-onto-main workaround instead of a clean rebase.
Multiple rounds of greptile comments required repeated merge+fix+push cycles, each discovering new issues (4 separate rounds on PR #465).
The `moltis-agents` dependency was missing from httpd's Cargo.toml, causing a compile error only discovered after threading the browser tool type through multiple structs.
The `install` feature gate on skills/src/lib.rs caused a false-positive greptile comment; restoring it broke compilation because main had already added unconditional users.
The metrics interval regression (10s instead of 30s) and cleanup counter mismatch (360 instead of 120) were introduced during merge conflict resolution and only caught by review.
23 merge conflicts in the first main merge required parallel agent resolution across multiple file groups.
The session-header.js merge conflict resolution silently chose the wrong condition, only caught by E2E test failure later.
open_items: The 4 sandbox E2E test failures (missing 'Refresh' button / container runtime unavailability) are pre-existing on main and were not fixed in this PR.

The `install` feature on moltis-skills has no corresponding Cargo.toml feature entry — the guard was dead code; this inconsistency between the feature gate pattern and actual usage should be cleaned up.
The release.yml redundant build removal saved CI time but the root cause (why builds were duplicated per-job) was not documented.
The squash commit approach used to recover from the filter-repo history rewrite means this PR loses granular commit history for the httpd extraction work.
